1.11a: Instructions must be accessed via LMS Conveyancer Zone website
Mortgage offer and supporting documents are included in the instruction
Queries must be raised through Secure Link via LMS Conveyancer Zone or via email at conveyancing@generationhome.com
1.11b: Refer to contact details and procedures in 1.11a
1.7: Lending to non-owner mortgagors is permitted in certain circumstances
Enquiries must be sent via Secure Link (LMS) or conveyancing@generationhome.com
1.14: A firm cannot act if the fee earner or their immediate family is the seller
A firm may act if a separate fee earner or partner represents the seller and no conflict of interest exists
1.15: A firm cannot act if the fee earner or their immediate family is the borrower
A firm may act if a separate fee earner or partner represents the borrower and the lender, provided no conflict of interest exists
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is not required unless the firm is not registered with a regulatory body
3.1.4: Notify the lender via the contact details in 1.11a if the seller is unrepresented
Lender will not proceed if the seller does not have legal representation
3.1.5: No alternative documents are accepted for identity verification
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is not required unless the firm is not registered with a regulatory body
3.2.4: Notify the lender via the contact details in 1.11a if the seller is unrepresented
Lender will not proceed if the seller does not have legal representation
4.1: A valuation report is provided with the offer only if a physical or desktop valuation was performed
4.3: Report incorrect valuation assumptions via Secure Link in LMS Conveyancer Zone
4.5a: Submit re-inspection requests via Secure Link in LMS Conveyancer Zone
4.5b: Certificate of title link is provided within the mortgage offer
Submission issues must be reported via Secure Link (LMS) or conveyancing@generationhome.com
5.1.1: Report if seller has owned property for less than 6 months via Secure Link (LMS)
5.2.1: Report if seller is not the registered proprietor via Secure Link (LMS)
5.4.4: Do not send environmental or contaminated land reports unless essential to report an adverse entry
Borrower must disclose entries to insurer; insurance must be on standard terms without onerous exclusions
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the agent has professional indemnity insurance and belongs to a trade body adhering to The Property Ombudsman scheme
5.4.6: Search validation indemnity insurance is accepted if searches are 6-12 months old and recommended by the conveyancer
No-search indemnity insurance is accepted for remortgages if recommended by the conveyancer
Indemnity limit must equal or exceed the property value
5.5.3a: Report planning breaches or unsatisfied conditions via Secure Link (LMS)
5.5.3b: Original or copy planning permissions are not required
5.5.3c: Original or copy building regulation consents are not required
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required
5.5.4: Report restrictions affecting marketability or value via Secure Link (LMS)
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted at lender discretion if they represent less than 20% of total floor area and are common in the local area
5.7.1b: Lender does not lend on freehold flats
5.7.1c: Report flying freehold or freehold flat titles via Secure Link (LMS)
5.8.1: Share of freehold is accepted for buildings with up to four flats if the freehold is split equally between dwellings
5.8.5: Security comprising one of two leasehold flats where owners cross-hold freehold reversions is accepted if all units have long leases
5.10.1: Report restrictions on use via Secure Link (LMS)
5.13.1: Report second charges or non-standard funding of purchase balance via Secure Link (LMS)
Deposits from unsecured credit or personal borrowing are not accepted
Gifted deposits from relatives (per Family Law Act Section 63) are discretionary
Conveyancer must report donor name, relationship, amount, and occupancy status
Gifts from cohabitees of the borrower or cohabitees of family members require specific written instruction
Conveyancer must retain a signed donor letter confirming the gift is non-repayable, unconditional, and grants no interest in the property
Bankruptcy searches are required for all parties contributing to the purchase balance
Lender does not consent to second charges
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 85 years at mortgage commencement and 50 years remaining at end of term
5.14.9: For new leases under Leasehold Reform Act 2022, ground rent must be maximum one peppercorn per year
Ground rent escalation linked to RPI or similar index is acceptable without referral
Refer to lender if ground rent review period is less than 10 years
Refer to lender if ground rent doubles in less than 20 years
Refer to lender if ground rent exceeds 0.1% of property value
Refer to lender if ground rent is linked to Open Market Value
5.14.10: Report service charges exceeding 1% of property value or significant planned increases via Secure Link (LMS)
5.14.11: Unsatisfactory lease terms should be varied with the landlord
Indemnity insurance for lease terms is discretionary and requires referral via 1.11a
5.14.12: Ground rent or service charge receipts are not required
5.14.13: Notice of service evidence is not required
5.14.15a: Report absentee or insolvent landlords via Secure Link (LMS)
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absentee/insolvent landlords is acceptable for leasehold properties with 6 or fewer flats
Absentee/insolvent landlords are not acceptable if the building contains 7 or more flats
5.14.17b: Report incorrect Building Safety Act 2022 remediation assumptions or unidentified remediation needs before submitting Certificate of Title
5.14.17c: For remortgages, report incorrect Building Safety Act 2022 assumptions or remediation needs before submitting Certificate of Title
5.15.2a: Historic rent charges under £200/year uncollected for 12+ years are acceptable; otherwise, indemnity insurance is required
Collected rent charges are acceptable if statutory remedies under LPA S121 are excluded, or the residents own the management company, or a 2-month mortgagee notification clause exists
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates are not required
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms are not required
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association are not required
5.16.2: Contact lender via 1.11a if unable to certify search entry non-relation
5.17.5a: Notify lender via 1.11a if a Power of Attorney is being used
5.17.5b: Notify lender via 1.11a if a Statutory Declaration of non-revocation of Power of Attorney exists
5.19.1: Notify lender via 1.11a for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership
Lending usually limited to cases where borrower owns 100% at completion
5.20.1: Report solar panel roof space leases not meeting UK Finance minimum requirements via 1.11a
5.20.3: Solar PV roof leases must meet UKF minimum standards; case cannot proceed if standard is not met and lease cannot be varied
Lender does not require a copy of the lease
5.20.4: Disclose Green Deal plans via 1.11a, including work details, balance, payments, term, guarantees, and EPC
6.1.3: Notify lender via 1.11a if borrower does not proceed with offer
6.4.4: Notify lender of incentives not in instructions or valuation via Secure Link (LMS)
Provide UK Finance Disclosure of Incentives Form only if different from the version in the valuation
6.4.5: Report if firm lacks control over all purchase money via 1.11a
6.5.1: Report if vacant possession is not provided via 1.11a
6.6.1: Lender does not consent to letting on residential mortgages
6.6.2: Lender does not consent to letting on residential mortgages
6.6.4: Lender does not lend on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO)
6.7.1: Accepted 10-year warranties: NHBC, Zurich Municipal (Newbuild/Rebuild), Premier Guarantee, Build Assure (FMB), Building Life Plans, Buildzone, LABC Warranty, ICW, One Guarantee, Castle Ten/Checkmate, Global Home Warranties, Protek, Q Assure, Advantage HCI
6.7.2: Send full warranty documents after completion as per section 14.2.2
6.7.3: Send building standards indemnity assignments after completion as per section 14.2.2
6.7.4: Properties without a listed warranty or Professional Consultant's Certificate (PCC) require reporting via Secure Link (LMS)
6.7.6: PCC must be in standard UK Finance format
Supply PCC after completion as per section 14.2.2
6.8.1: Report unadopted roads/sewers without agreement/bond via 1.11a
Transfer/lease must include developer maintenance covenants and adoption obligations
Private drainage must be within boundaries or have formal rights, and comply with General Binding Rules
6.10.2: Lender does not agree to retentions or phased fund releases
6.11.1: Report redevelopment or road proposals via 1.11a
6.12.1: Report pre-emption rights, resale restrictions, or options via 1.11a
6.13.1: Report undischarged improvement or repair grants via 1.11a
7.3: Signed Deed of Consent and Postponement required for all occupants aged 17+
Form must be provided prior to submission of Certificate of Title
Independent Legal Advice (ILA) documentation for occupiers is sent directly to the occupier's solicitor
7.4: Report doubts about accuracy of information via 1.11a
8.1: Firm may only advise third parties if a separate fee earner does so and no conflict of interest exists
9.1: Include indemnity policy details in Certificate of Title
Do not send policy documents to lender; provide them to the borrower
9.2: Indemnity limit must equal property value unless otherwise agreed in writing
10.2a: Advance paid via CHAPS on the working day before the completion date
10.2b: Minimum notice for advance: 5 clear working days
10.3: Standard deductions are detailed in the mortgage offer
10.7: Delayed completion requires a new Certificate of Title
Funds must be returned via CHAPS within one working day of scheduled completion
Notify lender via Secure Link (LMS) once funds are returned
10.9: Notify lender of delays via Secure Link (LMS); submit new Certificate of Title
10.10: Funds must be returned within one working day if completion is delayed
10.11: Interest is charged at the mortgage rate from the date of release until funds are recredited to the lender's account
12.3.1: Lender does not agree to retentions or phased fund releases
14.1.4: Lender does not require Form CH2 applications; no obligation for further advances
14.1.5: Do not send original mortgage deeds
Certified copies must be uploaded via LMS within 7 working days of completion
Electronic execution per Land Registry Practice Guide 82 is accepted
14.2.1: Registration and post-completion documents must be sent via LMS Conveyancer Zone
14.2.2: Required post-completion documents (within 7 working days): Certified copy Mortgage Deed, Deposit Booster Agreement, Home Agreement, Power of Attorney/Statutory Declaration, Occupier's Consent and Postponement Deed, New Build Warranty/PCC, Official Copy of Title Register
16.1.1: Refer to 1.11a for title document contact details
16.3.1: Lender has no standard form of transfer or deed of covenant
16.3.4: Transfer of equity document does not need to be sent
16.3.7b: Attestation clause: Executed as a Deed for and on behalf of Imagine Mortgages Limited by a duly authorised signatory
16.4.1: Applications for consent to let: hello@generationhome.com
16.5.2: Confirm impact of deeds/agreements via Secure Link (LMS) or conveyancing@generationhome.com
16.5.3a: Send deeds of variation via Secure Link (LMS) or conveyancing@generationhome.com
16.5.3b: Send deeds of rectification via Secure Link (LMS) or conveyancing@generationhome.com
16.5.3c: Send deeds of easement via Secure Link (LMS) or conveyancing@generationhome.com
16.5.3d: Send option agreements via Secure Link (LMS) or conveyancing@generationhome.com
17.2.1a: Discharge and remittance instructions are provided in the redemption statement
17.2.1b: Lender issues electronic DS1 to HM Land Registry; conveyancer does not need to request discharge
